title: Presigned Browser Uploads, POST Policies, and CORS · Contract Triage
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible least-privilege direct-upload session contract with one identity, byte, checksum, policy, cache, ownership, cancellation, cleanup, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in least-privilege direct-upload session contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Create a one-object upload intent with tenant, generated key, expected size, type, checksum, expiry, status, and replay policy before signing.
Presign PUT with exact region, expiry, key, checksum, content type, encryption, and required header parity.
Use a POST policy when browser-enforced key, size, metadata, and field conditions are required and validate the completed object independently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Upload Session Intent
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 3885 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to finalize a direct upload against intent while separating CORS from IAM authorization; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Create a one-object upload intent with tenant, generated key, expected size, type, checksum, expiry, status, and replay policy before signing.

Inspect the Upload Session Intent trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Upload Session Intent.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Upload Session Intent, keep Upload Session Intent connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 8826 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to finalize a direct upload against intent while separating CORS from IAM authorization; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Finalize by matching intent to HEAD or event evidence, checksum, version, size, owner, scan result, and idempotent state transition.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Direct Upload Finalization has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Direct Upload Finalization, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Direct Upload Finalization precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. options.text: Declare S3, CloudFront, and Go stakeholder outcome, file and object identity, owner, state, and byte, time, concurrency, cache, and cost budgets for the debug Direct Upload Finalization case
Predict S3, CloudFront, and Go authorization, custody, transfer, integrity, cache, failure, cancellation, cleanup, rollback, and recovery outcomes for the debug Direct Upload Finalization case
Run the smallest deterministic S3, CloudFront, and Go storage decision model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the debug Direct Upload Finalization case
Inject one changed S3, CloudFront, and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup and recovery, and state controlled AWS transfer limits for the debug Direct Upload Finalization case

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Direct Upload Finalization
